title: Glass Onion
logline: Detective Benoit Blanc travels to a Greek island to investigate a murder mystery weekend that turns deadly when the tech billionaire host's former business partner is shot, only to discover she was already murdered days earlier and her twin sister has been posing as her to expose the killer.
protagonist: Helen Brand (posing as her murdered twin sister Andi)
antagonistOrConflict: Miles Bron, tech billionaire who murdered Andi and frames the weekend murder mystery
themes: The emptiness behind wealth and genius

actBreaks.description: Helen is shot on the island grounds, ending the murder mystery weekend and launching the investigation into what really happened.

validation.ruling: Scene #51 (p73-74, 54%) functions as Act 1 break: the lights come back on revealing 'Andi' dead with a chest wound and Blanc standing over the body—this is the major plot twist that fundamentally changes the story's direction from a weekend game into a murder investigation, forcing all characters into new, higher-stakes conflict with no possibility of returning to the pre-break state.

actBreaks.description: The narrative returns to the island in present time after extensive flashbacks revealing Helen's true identity and mission, shifting into active investigation mode.

validation.ruling: Scene #89 (p103-105, 76%) functions as Act 2 break: Birdie reveals to Peg that Miles will pay her $30 million to take full responsibility for the sweatshop scandal, declaring 'He's my only lifeline'—this completes Helen's investigation by revealing the final suspect's motive and Miles's hold over everyone, fundamentally shifting the story from gathering evidence to the final confrontation phase where Helen must act on what she's learned.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a triptych mystery that plays with audience perception through strategic withholding and revelation. The first act presents a seemingly straightforward murder mystery weekend that ends with an apparent shooting. The second act rewinds completely, revealing that 'Andi' is actually her twin sister Helen, and replays the island events with new context while intercutting flashbacks to Andi's actual murder days earlier. The third act returns to linear time for the confrontation and climax. The structure mirrors its central metaphor—the glass onion—by appearing complex and layered while actually being transparent once you know where to look. It's a magic trick that depends on misdirection in the first act, revelation in the second, and cathartic destruction in the third.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The opening 15 scenes establish the ensemble through rapid crosscutting during the puzzle box sequence, creating momentum before the 40-scene island arrival and setup. The pace deliberately slows during Miles's tour and dinner (scenes 26-35) to build unease. The murder mystery game accelerates through scenes 36-50 with mounting tension. Scene 51 provides a hard stop. The flashback structure of Act 2 (scenes 53-88) allows for controlled information release while maintaining urgency. The final act (scenes 106-150) is a sustained 45-scene sprint of revelation, confrontation, and literal destruction.

incitingIncident.description: Blanc asks Lionel about the tension surrounding a woman named Andi, learning she co-founded Alpha with Miles but was legally cut out completely.

validation.ruling: Scene #16 is merely a title card transition over water with no story content. Scene #20 (p21-25, 15-19%) is the true inciting incident: Blanc and the group arrive at the jetty where they board Miles's boat to his private island, forcing all characters into the isolated setting where the central mystery will unfold—this is the irreversible commitment that shatters their ordinary worlds and establishes the main conflict.

midpoint.description: Whiskey tells Helen this is her second event with the group, complains that Duke treats her like arm candy and they all ignore her, and reveals she's building her brand through Duke's channel but is uncertain about his harder-right man-dom content if she wants to get into politics eventually.

validation.ruling: Scene #72 is casual conversation between Whiskey and Helen with no transformation. Scene #76 (p92-94, 68-70%) is the true midpoint: Helen eavesdrops underwater as both Lionel and Claire confess they've already signed off on Miles's dangerous plans despite knowing Andi was right, revealing that all the suspects have bet everything on Miles and will be ruined if he fails—this is the key revelation that shifts Helen from reactive snooping to understanding the full stakes and conspiracy against her sister.

climax.description: Everyone looks at Miles as Lionel asks why he kept the envelope, Miles looks confused, Helen pulls out the original napkin with Andi's handwriting and the bar's embossed name, Miles ignites a lighter and burns the napkin to ash in a fraction of a second, then the Mona Lisa glass case comes down and Helen smashes the painting with the Klear scepter, causing Miles to scream as she throws the scepter into the Klear contraption which explodes.

validation.ruling: Scene #146 is the aftermath of the climax, showing the destruction already complete. Scene #144 (p127-133, 94-99%) is the true climax: Helen confronts Miles with the original napkin proving Andi's ownership, Miles burns it in desperation, then Helen smashes the Mona Lisa and throws the Klear scepter into the fuel source causing the massive explosion—this is the peak confrontation where the protagonist directly faces the antagonist, resolves the main story question, and demonstrates her character arc by choosing destruction over justice.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. SUBURBAN HOUSE - DAY 2
scenes.summary: A courier delivers a package to Claire at her suburban Connecticut home.

scenes.heading: 3 INT. DEBELLA HOME 3
scenes.summary: Claire brings the mysterious box inside to her husband Devon and rushes to prepare for a CNN interview.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. AEROSPACE FACTORY 4
scenes.summary: A worker rolls a cart through an empty aerospace factory while Claire's CNN interview plays on an unattended TV.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM 5
scenes.summary: Lionel sits in a conference room facing frustrated scientists on Zoom who tell him to say no to Miles Bron's insane whims.

scenes.heading: 6 EXT. THE DAKOTA - MANHATTAN - DAY 6
scenes.summary: Exterior establishing shot of the Dakota apartment building in Manhattan.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. APARTMENT 7
scenes.summary: Birdie Jay lounges in her apartment filled with partying people during the pandemic, watching Claire's interview and complaining of boredom.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. DEBELLA HOME 8
scenes.summary: Claire and Devon examine the mysterious box on speakerphone with Lionel, who identifies the wood grain pattern as a stereogram puzzle.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. THREE CAR GARAGE - DAY 9
scenes.summary: Duke Cody broadcasts a YouTube video defending himself against accusations while his mother solves the stereogram box puzzle off-screen.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. DEBELLA HOME / CONFERENCE ROOM / APARTMENT 10
scenes.summary: The group on split-screen speakerphone works together to solve the stereogram puzzle, revealing intricate mechanisms inside the box.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. DUKE'S KITCHEN - DAY 11
scenes.summary: Duke and his mother discuss the opened box while the group on speakerphone discovers it contains an invitation to Miles Bron's private Greek island.
scenes.turningPointNote: The invitation to the island sets the entire story in motion and brings all characters together.

scenes.heading: 12 INT. DUKE'S KITCHEN 12
scenes.summary: Duke excitedly prepares to pack for the trip while Whiskey asks about the box.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM 13
scenes.summary: Lionel leans back with the invitation and turns off his monitors.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. APARTMENT 14
scenes.summary: Birdie dances around her party in excitement about the invitation.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. GARAGE 15
scenes.summary: An unidentified woman stares at the unsolved box in a darkened garage, then returns with a hammer and smashes it open to retrieve the invitation.

scenes.heading: 16 INT. BENOIT BLANC'S APARTMENT - DAY 16
scenes.summary: Benoit Blanc sits in his bathtub playing Among Us with celebrity friends who vote him out, frustrated that he doesn't understand the game.

scenes.heading: 17 SHIMMERING WATER 17
scenes.summary: Opening title card appears over shimmering water.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. GRECIAN PORT TOWN - DAY 18
scenes.summary: Blanc waits alone on a jetty in Greece until Lionel, Claire, Birdie, and Peg arrive separately, all keeping distance and ignoring Blanc.

scenes.heading: 19 EXT. GRECIAN PORT TOWN STREETS - DAY 19
scenes.summary: Duke roars through Greek streets on a motorcycle with Whiskey, who fires a gun into the air.

scenes.heading: 20 EXT. JETTY 20
scenes.summary: Duke greets the group on the jetty with Whiskey while everyone reacts with confusion to Blanc's presence, and an efficient man directs them to a boat.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. BOAT - AT SEA - DAY 21
scenes.summary: Blanc asks Lionel about the tension surrounding a woman named Andi, learning she co-founded Alpha with Miles but was legally cut out completely.

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. GREEK ISLAND - DAY 22
scenes.summary: Aerial shot shows the boat approaching a small Greek island with a single compound.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. BEACH - DAY 23
scenes.summary: A glass dock with political art magically rises from the sea as the boat approaches.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. BOAT 24
scenes.summary: Lionel asks Captain Andino about the dock and the island's name in Greek.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. BEACH 25
scenes.summary: Miles Bron greets his arriving friends on the beach, playing guitar and hugging them warmly, then tosses Paul McCartney's guitar onto his chair.

scenes.heading: 26 EXT. VILLA - COMPOUND 26
scenes.summary: The group walks through the compound and sees the main building, a massive glass orb structure, while Miles describes it and Andi joins them.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. THE GLASS ONION - DAY 27
scenes.summary: Miles shows Blanc his office in the glass onion and his blue Porsche on the roof, then reveals he did not invite Blanc to the island.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Miles didn't invite Blanc introduces a central mystery and raises immediate suspicion about who did.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. POOL - DAY 28
scenes.summary: At the pool, the group socializes while Blanc joins Miles, who discusses his murder mystery game plans and Duke livestreams about sweatshops.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 29
scenes.summary: Claire watches Andi walk away into the greenery and tells Lionel something is off and she doesn't like it.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. ZEN GARDEN - DAY 30
scenes.summary: Peg begs Miles not to make Birdie issue a statement about Bangladesh that would destroy her reputation and Peg's career.

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 31
scenes.summary: Duke spies through a window and sees Miles kissing Whiskey on a bed while Blanc secretly watches Duke's enraged reaction.

scenes.heading: 32 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - EVENING 32
scenes.summary: The glass onion structure glows softly as evening arrives and the hourly chime sounds.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. ATRIUM - EVENING 33
scenes.summary: Miles greets guests in the atrium with personalized drinks and snacks, discussing each person's favorite beverage while Blanc tries hot sauce.

scenes.heading: 34 INT. DINING AREA - MINUTES LATER 34
scenes.summary: Miles explains the murder mystery game rules at dinner, then Blanc immediately solves it by identifying Whiskey as the killer using the crossbow.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. THE GLASS ONION - EVENING 35
scenes.summary: Miles angrily confronts Blanc in his office for ruining the expensive murder mystery game he hired Gillian Flynn to create.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 36
scenes.summary: The group drinks heavily after dinner while Duke's phone alerts repeatedly, and Andi announces she's going to bed early.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. RADIO ROOM - CONTINUOUS 37
scenes.summary: Lionel discovers via radio that the boat cannot return until morning due to the malfunctioning Banksy dock.

scenes.heading: 38 INT. LOUNGE AREA 38
scenes.summary: Duke suddenly collapses and dies after drinking from Miles's glass, and Blanc discovers Duke was poisoned.
scenes.turningPointNote: Duke's death transforms the weekend from a game into a real murder investigation with everyone trapped on the island.

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 39
scenes.summary: The entire compound goes dark as the lighthouse beam sweeps across the grounds.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 40
scenes.summary: In pitch darkness, Miles panics and whimpers while the group calls out to each other, unable to see.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 41
scenes.summary: Miles runs through dark hallways while Andi spots him and Blanc calls out for her.

scenes.heading: 42 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 42
scenes.summary: Peg turns on her phone flashlight in the atrium and discovers she is completely alone.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 43
scenes.summary: Miles moves slowly through dark hallways, barely breathing and listening to shadows around him.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT 44
scenes.summary: Blanc enters the kitchen and sees someone exit, then discovers a knife missing from the butcher's block.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 45
scenes.summary: Blanc runs into Andi outside and tells her Duke is dead, asking if she took Duke's gun.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. HALL - NIGHT 46
scenes.summary: A gloved hand holds Duke's gun in a dark hallway and takes careful aim at Blanc and Andi through the window.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 47
scenes.summary: A gunshot rings out, the window shatters, and Andi's body is thrown out of frame as if struck.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andi is shot, creating a second murder and eliminating a key character who holds crucial information.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. THE GLASS ONION 48
scenes.summary: The gunshot echoes through the empty halls and rooms of the glass onion.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. ATRIUM 49
scenes.summary: The Mona Lisa's protective glass slides down as voices echo through distant halls and everyone searches for each other.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. GROUNDS 50
scenes.summary: The lights come back on revealing Blanc standing over Andi's body with a chest wound while everyone watches from windows above.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 51
scenes.summary: Blanc orders Peg to radio for boats immediately while the group struggles to understand the murders, and Blanc refuses to explain until he can prove it.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. TERRACE - DAY 53
scenes.summary: Blanc and Helen meet on his terrace where she removes her mask and reveals her true identity as Helen Brand, Andi's twin sister from Alabama.
scenes.turningPointNote: Helen's true identity is revealed to Blanc, establishing the central deception that will drive the investigation forward.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. MORGUE - FLASHBACK 54
scenes.summary: Helen identifies her twin sister Andi's body at the morgue after Andi's apparent suicide by carbon monoxide poisoning in her garage.

scenes.heading: 55 EXT. TERRACE 55
scenes.summary: Helen tells Blanc about her sister Cassandra Brand's transformation from their childhood together to becoming a successful businesswoman, then describes receiving the call about Andi's death and cleaning out her house while grieving.

scenes.heading: 56 SHIMMERING WATER 56
scenes.summary: The camera tilts up from shimmering water at night to reveal the next location.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. GRECIAN PORT TOWN - NIGHT 57
scenes.summary: The camera pushes in on an adorable dockside hotel lit up at night in a Grecian port town.

scenes.heading: 58 EXT. HOTEL TERRACE - NIGHT 58
scenes.summary: Helen, disguised as Andi, meets Blanc at a hotel terrace where he coaches her on how to behave the next day, instructing her to arrive late, stay cold, use Andi's voice and posture, and avoid drinking.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. THE GLASS ONION BAR - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 59
scenes.summary: In flashback at the Glass Onion bar, young Andi gathers with Birdie, Duke, Claire, and Lionel as Helen explains in voiceover that Andi was the group's leader who saw potential in each of them before finding Miles.

scenes.heading: 60 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 60
scenes.summary: Blanc asks Helen what it means that Andi found Miles.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. THE GLASS ONION BAR - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 61
scenes.summary: In flashback, the group huddles at the bar discussing Miles's aspirations for immortality while Andi convinces them to humor him, leading to a time-cut montage showing Miles getting each of them career opportunities.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 62
scenes.summary: Helen explains to Blanc that Andi and Miles created Alpha based on a napkin idea, then two years ago Miles became obsessed with launching a new hydrogen fuel called Klear after meeting a Norwegian chemist.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. BOARD ROOM - FLASHBACK - DAY 63
scenes.summary: In an Alpha boardroom, Andi refuses to sign the Klear contract, telling Miles the fuel could literally blow up the world and threatening to walk away from the company.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andi's refusal to approve Klear and threat to leave the company establishes the core conflict that led to her being cut out and eventually murdered.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 64
scenes.summary: Helen tells Blanc that Andi walked away, then discovered Miles's lawyers had worked the contracts to cut her out completely, leading her to sue based on intellectual ownership of the napkin idea she didn't keep.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 65
scenes.summary: In court, Claire, Duke, and Birdie each testify that Miles wrote the napkin idea while Andi stands and furiously accuses Claire of lying, demanding she look her in the eye.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. ANDI'S LIVING ROOM - FLASHBACK - DAY 66
scenes.summary: After losing the trial, Andi stares dead-eyed at a Forbes article about Miles and his napkin, then goes on a destructive rampage through her living room, throwing her iPad and flipping furniture.

scenes.heading: 67 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 67
scenes.summary: Blanc calls Miles's napkin story a bald-faced fabrication and Helen confirms it was a damn lie that worked.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. ANDI'S LIVING ROOM - FLASHBACK - DAY 68
scenes.summary: Mid-destruction, Andi stops when she spots her old copy of The Innovator's Dilemma in the pile, picks it up, and the real napkin falls out from the dust sleeve.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of the real napkin in Andi's handwriting provides the proof that could destroy Miles and the disruptors, establishing the motive for murder.

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. HOTEL - NIGHT 69
scenes.summary: Blanc explains they need to find whose motive was strong enough to commit murder and determine everyone's whereabouts on the night of Andi's death, while Helen draws a grid with four suspect names and columns for motive and opportunity.

scenes.heading: 70 EXT. SEA - MORNING 70
scenes.summary: On the ferry to Bron's island, Helen stands on the back deck sick from the boat while Duke and Birdie watch her from below, with Birdie telling her she shouldn't be there.

scenes.heading: 71 EXT. BEACH - DAY 71
scenes.summary: Blanc stops to tie his shoelace while Helen hangs back with him, and he quietly instructs her to snoop while Miles pulls him aside, warning her that Duke and Birdie may be onto her.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. ANDI'S ROOM - DAY 72
scenes.summary: Helen puts her bag down in Andi's room and psychs herself up to start snooping.

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. GROUNDS 73
scenes.summary: Helen wanders the grounds in a bathing suit looking for something to snoop when Whiskey approaches, offers her hard kombucha, and they walk together to the pool while making awkward small talk about Whiskey's necklace.

scenes.heading: 74 EXT. GROUNDS - CONTINUOUS 74
scenes.summary: Whiskey tells Helen this is her second event with the group, complains that Duke treats her like arm candy and they all ignore her, and reveals she's building her brand through Duke's channel but is uncertain about his harder-right man-dom content if she wants to get into politics eventually.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. HERB GARDEN 75
scenes.summary: Helen emerges from the herb garden lost and frustrated, takes another pull from the kombucha, then hears a gunshot and looks in that direction.

scenes.heading: 76 EXT. POOL - CONTINUOUS 76
scenes.summary: Duke fires his pistol at the pool while Lionel and Claire swim to the deep end for privacy, where Claire reveals she signed off on the power plant two weeks ago and Lionel admits he signed off on putting Klear in the manned mission despite knowing Andi was right about its dangers, as Helen lurks below listening.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. BATHROOM - LATER 77
scenes.summary: Blanc and Helen huddle in a bathroom where he praises her snooping skills and explains that both Lionel and Claire have bet everything on Miles and will be ruined if he fails, while Helen drinks another kombucha and ticks off their motive boxes.

scenes.heading: 78 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - HYPOTHETICAL - EVENING 78
scenes.summary: Duke knocks on Andi's door with his motorcycle nearby and she answers as Blanc's voiceover suggests the killer came to apologize and make amends.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. ANDI'S KITCHEN - HYPOTHETICAL - NIGHT 79
scenes.summary: In Andi's kitchen, Blanc's voiceover describes the murder as nonviolent and gentle as we see Lionel slip powder in Andi's tea, then Andi fade from consciousness and sink to the table with Claire watching coldly.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. ANDI'S GARAGE - HYPOTHETICAL - EVENING 80
scenes.summary: Birdie turns on the car engine and lowers the windows with Andi slumped in the passenger seat as Blanc's voiceover concludes they're all capable of murder.

scenes.heading: 81 INT. BATHROOM 81
scenes.summary: In the bathroom, Blanc pours out Helen's hard kombucha and fills it with water, then realizes she's had two or three bottles of nine percent alcohol kombucha and is drunk, while Helen insists they need to push the suspects for opportunity.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. POOL 82
scenes.summary: Helen listens to Miles's speech about disruption at the pool, then takes a long pull from the kombucha, straightens her posture, and claps to interrupt him, drawing everyone's attention including Blanc's alarmed look.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. GROUNDS - AFTERNOON 83
scenes.summary: Helen bursts away from the pool onto a path, steadying herself as the trees spin, then Claire runs to catch up and confronts her, asking what she's doing and why she couldn't look Claire in the eye in court, before Duke joins them and Claire asks about the email Helen supposedly sent.

scenes.heading: 84 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - EVENING 84
scenes.summary: In flashback, Claire's Prius arrives at Andi's house at the same time as Lionel's Tesla, joining Duke who is already pounding on the door, and Duke spots and smashes a spider on the wall while they wait.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT. GROUNDS 85
scenes.summary: Claire tells Helen they knocked and waited for an hour before leaving, then Birdie checked later with the same result, and when Helen asks what time they arrived and tries to clarify the timeline, Claire demands to know what Helen is planning and insists they need to talk about the email.

scenes.heading: 86 EXT. GARDEN 86
scenes.summary: Helen stumbles away from the confrontation, swoons from the kombucha, then sees flashing red lights and a soothing robot voice warning about the smokeless garden before calling out to Blanc who dashes from the foliage looking panicked.

scenes.heading: 87 INT. GYM - DAY 87
scenes.summary: In the gym, Helen recounts Claire's story to Blanc while pounding Gatorade, and they tick off opportunity boxes for all four suspects since Duke came early, Birdie came late, and Claire and Lionel could have killed Andi before the others arrived, as a fitness video screen behind them begins playing a workout with Serena Williams.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. GROUNDS 88
scenes.summary: Duke kneels in the foliage watching through a window as Miles and Whiskey make out and fall onto a bed, with Whiskey asking if Miles will do it for her and pinning him down, while Helen and Blanc creep up behind Duke until Helen snaps a twig.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT. BIRDIE'S VILLA - AFTERNOON 89
scenes.summary: Peg storms out of Birdie's villa with Birdie chasing after her, and when they're gone Helen dashes up, grabs Birdie's bag from inside the patio, and retrieves her phone which is still recording.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. ANDI'S VILLA - MINUTES LATER 90
scenes.summary: Blanc and Helen listen to the phone recording where Peg demands to know what Miles meant by saying something is Birdie's only chance.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. BIRDIE'S VILLA - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON 91
scenes.summary: In flashback, Birdie tells Peg she's going to sign a statement taking total responsibility for the sweatshop scandal, then hands Peg her secret phone showing an email from two years ago where the contractor warned her about the Bangladesh factory being a notorious sweatshop and Birdie replied to proceed anyway.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. ANDI'S VILLA 92
scenes.summary: Helen stops the recording and glumly ticks off the motive boxes for Birdie and Duke, completing the grid, then Blanc deduces that whoever killed Andi took the envelope to show Miles what they did for him rather than destroy it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blanc's realization that the killer would have given the envelope to Miles provides a concrete objective for Helen's search and shifts the investigation strategy.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 93
scenes.summary: Miles offers Andi a whiskey soda at the gathering but Blanc shakes his head and Helen stashes the drink behind a statue, then the glass slides down in front of the Mona Lisa as time cuts forward to Helen enduring Duke's rant.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 94
scenes.summary: Helen sprints through the grounds as soon as she's out of sight.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. CLAIRE'S VILLA - NIGHT 95
scenes.summary: Helen bursts into Claire's villa and tears it apart searching for the envelope, finding clothes, pot, iPads, laptops, and a vibrator, leaving the room looking like a tornado hit it, but finds no envelope.

scenes.heading: 96 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 96
scenes.summary: Helen sprints through the grounds with the glowing glass onion structure looming above as time ticks away.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 97
scenes.summary: Miles cheers as Birdie spins in her rainbow dress in the atrium.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. BIRDIE'S VILLA - NIGHT 98
scenes.summary: Helen searches Birdie's villa finding dresses, beach wraps, prescription pills, and a dog-eared copy of The Fountainhead, then feels her phone buzz in her pocket.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 99
scenes.summary: Duke grasps his throat after downing his drink in the atrium.
scenes.turningPointNote: Duke's sudden physical distress after drinking marks the second murder attempt and escalates the immediate danger, transforming the investigation into an active crisis.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. LIONEL'S VILLA 100
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 101 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 101
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 102 INT. DEROL'S VILLA - NIGHT 102
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 103 INT. DUKE'S VILLA - NIGHT 103
scenes.summary: Helen runs into Duke's villa and discovers dozens of messages informing her that Cassandra Brand has been reported dead of apparent suicide, then searches Duke's room but finds no envelope.
scenes.turningPointNote: Helen learns her sister's death has been publicly reported as suicide, raising the stakes of her investigation while she's still undercover

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. GROUNDS - NIGHT 104
scenes.summary: Helen runs across the dark grounds toward the glass onion, screaming in frustration.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 105
scenes.summary: Helen bursts through dark hallways, spots Miles turning a corner, then hears Blanc calling and runs toward his voice while Lionel emerges into the hall behind her.

scenes.heading: 106 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 106
scenes.summary: Helen runs into Blanc outside the glass onion and he tells her Duke is dead and asks if she found the envelope, which she hasn't, then Blanc says there's one more room to search and Helen realizes they already gave it to Miles.
scenes.turningPointNote: Duke's death is revealed and Helen realizes the envelope is in the glass onion, setting up the final confrontation

scenes.heading: 107 INT. HALLWAYS - NIGHT 107
scenes.summary: Helen silently moves through hallways and up stairs while distant voices of Miles and Blanc are heard.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. LOUNGE AREA - CONTINUOUS 108
scenes.summary: The suspects gather in the lounge as Blanc announces he can peel back the layers but only one person can tell them who killed Cassandra Brand, then mentions the glass onion.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 109
scenes.summary: Helen searches the glass office frantically while hearing Blanc's voice explaining that the glass onion seems mysterious but the center is in plain sight.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. LOUNGE AREA 110
scenes.summary: Blanc tells the group the case confounded him because he expected complexity and intelligence but that wasn't what this was.

scenes.heading: 111 INT. THE GLASS ONION 111
scenes.summary: Helen sees the framed napkin and photo of her sister with Miles, approaches it in a trance, pulls it off the wall, and the red envelope falls onto the floor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Helen finds the crucial evidence that can prove Andi's original idea

scenes.heading: 112 INT. LOUNGE AREA 112
scenes.summary: Blanc declares that the killer nearly struck his Achilles heel but he realized that 'embreathiate' is not a word, then spins to face Miles.

scenes.heading: 113 EXT. BEACH - FLASHBACK - DAY 113
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles saying 'Let's all embreathiate this moment' when they first arrived at the beach.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. LOUNGE AREA 114
scenes.summary: Blanc explains that 'embreathiate' is entirely made up and 'reclamation' is the wrong word.

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - FLASHBACK - DAY 115
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles saying 'This place is the reclamation of everything I've ever accomplished.'

scenes.heading: 116 INT. LOUNGE AREA 116
scenes.summary: Blanc says 'This entire day—'

scenes.heading: 117 INT. DISPLAY GARAGE - FLASHBACK - DAY 117
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles calling Blanc 'the predefinite detective.'

scenes.heading: 118 INT. LOUNGE AREA 118
scenes.summary: Blanc calls it 'a veritable minefield of malapropisms.'

scenes.heading: 119 EXT. POOL - FLASHBACK - DAY 119
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles saying 'the infraction point' while telling the Sweety Pants story.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. LOUNGE AREA 120
scenes.summary: Blanc says 'and factual errors!'

scenes.heading: 121 INT. DINING AREA - FLASHBACK - EVENING 121
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles saying they'll 'bask in the sun, swim in the Ionian Sea.'

scenes.heading: 122 INT. LOUNGE AREA 122
scenes.summary: Blanc points out the window and corrects that it's the Aegean sea, then lists Miles's failures and declares that Miles Bron is an idiot, making everyone shift uncomfortably.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blanc's central revelation that Miles is not a genius but an idiot reframes the entire case

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - EVENING 123
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Duke almost being hit by Miles in his distinctive blue Porsche as Miles leaves Andi's house, then Duke continues to the house.

scenes.heading: 124 EXT. POOL - FLASHBACK - DAY 124
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Duke saying 'Remember you almost pancaked me with it on the road that night at Andi's' while Miles cuts him off talking about Anderson Cooper's birthday in Spain.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 125
scenes.summary: Blanc identifies that Miles was at Andi's house that night and points out Whiskey's necklace was a birthday gift two weeks ago on May ninth, proving Miles was in New York, not Greece.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blanc establishes Miles was in New York the night of Andi's murder, not Greece as claimed

scenes.heading: 126 INT. AEROSPACE FACTORY - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON 126
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Lionel guiltily feeding Andi's email into a fax machine at the aerospace factory.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. NEW YORK PENTHOUSE - FLASHBACK - AFTERNOON 127
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles in a robe receiving the fax in his New York penthouse, reading it troubled, as Blanc narrates that the truth in Andi's hands was the one thing that could destroy Miles's empire of lies.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE ROAD - FLASHBACK - LATE AFTERNOON 128
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles driving his baby blue Porsche down a country road.

scenes.heading: 129 INT. ATRIUM 129
scenes.summary: Blanc stares at Miles and says he drove his signature baby blue Porsche to the scene of the crime.

scenes.heading: 130 EXT. ANDI'S HOUSE - FLASHBACK - LATE AFTERNOON 130
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles's car parked outside Andi's house.

scenes.heading: 131 INT. ANDI'S KITCHEN - FLASHBACK - LATE AFTERNOON 131
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles and Andi drinking tea at her kitchen table as he talks gently and she watches coldly, then her head droops as she realizes she's been poisoned while Miles becomes distracted by a spider he gently releases outside.
scenes.turningPointNote: The murder of Andi is shown in flashback—Miles poisoned her tea

scenes.heading: 132 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 132
scenes.summary: Miles looks blankly at Blanc and Helen with terrifying emptiness as Blanc explains Duke knew Miles was there that night and showed him something on his phone, then Blanc reveals a phone in Miles's jacket pocket and takes it.

scenes.heading: 133 INT. LOUNGE AREA - FLASHBACK - EARLIER THAT NIGHT 133
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Duke showing Miles the news story about Andi's death and telling Miles this changes everything, then asking about Alpha News in exchange for his silence.

scenes.heading: 134 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 134
scenes.summary: Helen asks if Miles keeps poison in his tooth and Blanc asks Birdie what's in her Cuban Breeze, leading Whiskey to say 'Oh god.'

scenes.heading: 135 INT. LOUNGE AREA - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 135
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles at the bar cart quietly adding pineapple juice to Duke's whiskey while Duke watches.

scenes.heading: 136 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 136
scenes.summary: Blanc exclaims that Miles put pineapple juice in Duke's whiskey despite Duke's deadly allergy, calling it so dumb, while the others read the news on their phones and realize Helen is Andi's twin sister, with Birdie finally understanding.
scenes.turningPointNote: The method of Duke's murder is revealed and the group discovers Helen's true identity

scenes.heading: 137 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT - FLASHBACK 137
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles in the dark with calculating eyes as Blanc narrates that Miles realized he had a house full of desperate people who all had reason to wish Andi harm.

scenes.heading: 138 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 138
scenes.summary: Blanc squints and says 'Furthermore' as if something is strange.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. LOUNGE AREA - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 139
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles grabbing Duke's gun in the dark confusion.

scenes.heading: 140 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 140
scenes.summary: Blanc narrows his eyes as if the words coming out of his mouth are strange.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. HALLWAYS - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 141
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles in dark halls pocketing Duke's phone with gun in hand as Blanc narrates that the lights had been turned off.

scenes.heading: 142 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 142
scenes.summary: Blanc exclaims 'OH MY GOD' as he realizes why all this sounded familiar.

scenes.heading: 143 INT. THE GLASS ONION - FLASHBACK - EVENING 143
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Blanc explaining to Miles that he gathered seven people with reason to wish him harm on a remote island and put the idea of murder in their heads, like putting a loaded gun on the table and turning off the lights.

scenes.heading: 144 INT. LOUNGE AREA 144
scenes.summary: Blanc turns to Miles unbelieving and says Miles stole the whole murder idea from him.

scenes.heading: 145 INT. HALLWAYS - FLASHBACK - NIGHT 145
scenes.summary: Flashback shows Miles raising Duke's gun and firing at Helen through the window, splintering the glass.
scenes.turningPointNote: Miles's attempt to murder Helen is revealed in flashback

scenes.heading: 146 INT. LOUNGE AREA - NIGHT 146
scenes.summary: Everyone looks at Miles as Lionel asks why he kept the envelope, Miles looks confused, Helen pulls out the original napkin with Andi's handwriting and the bar's embossed name, Miles ignites a lighter and burns the napkin to ash in a fraction of a second, then the Mona Lisa glass case comes down and Helen smashes the painting with the Klear scepter, causing Miles to scream as she throws the scepter into the Klear contraption which explodes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crucial evidence is destroyed, Helen destroys the Mona Lisa and triggers the Klear explosion in an act of irreversible destruction

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - NIGHT 147
scenes.summary: The glass onion structure explodes with glass and fire, then every villa goes up in flames like fireworks while Blanc lights a cigar in a lawn chair and passes the lighter to Derol.

scenes.heading: 148 INT. ATRIUM - NIGHT 148
scenes.summary: Everyone raises their heads dazed in the damaged atrium as fire jets from ruptured walls, Miles's baby blue Porsche falls from above and smashes in a flaming heap, Miles chokes back a sob, then Helen runs toward the Mona Lisa with everyone chasing and grabbing at her but she reaches it and smashes it.

scenes.heading: 149 EXT. THE GLASS ONION - MOMENTS LATER 149
scenes.summary: Everyone stumbles out choking as Miles turns on Helen and rants that she accomplished nothing, but Helen tells him his fuel just barbecued the Mona Lisa and this is the end of Miles Bron, which sinks in as his face goes slack and the others realize they can finally tell the truth.
scenes.turningPointNote: Miles realizes his empire is destroyed and the group decides to testify against him

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. BEACH - NIGHT 150
scenes.summary: Helen sits on the beach with Blanc as he asks if she got the son of a bitch, she confirms she did, he asks if she's ready to go home, and she sits with straightened posture gazing at the approaching police boats.
